category_title: Preschoolers with aggressive behaviors and social-emotional regulation difficulties
minimal_context: The student is asked to clean up toys and transition to the reading rug with the rest of the preschool class.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time to clean up the toys and transition to the reading rug.

STUDENT: No, I am not going.

TUTOR: We need to follow the classroom procedures and transition now.

STUDENT: I am not following the rules!

TUTOR: You need to come sit down with the rest of the class.

STUDENT: No! Do what I say or I will hurt you!

options.A: Model appropriate behaviors for the student during daily transitions and playtime using classroom puppets.
options.B: Implement a schedule of positive reinforcement consisting of praise, high fives, and a star chart leading to a favorite activity.
options.C: Establish the classroom rules and daily transition procedures in consensus with the entire preschool class.
options.D: Remain available without forcing interaction, first simply sitting near the student and then speaking quietly and gently touching their shoulder.
